query: Johor state election 2026 expected outcome, green wave PN Perikatan Nasional Malay voters, Barisan Nasional incumbency, Onn Hafiz popularity
answer:
I've completed my research on the Johor state election 2026. Here's a summary of my key findings:

**Timing & Context:** This is a snap election called ~10 months early by MB Onn Hafiz Ghazi. Polling day is 11 July 2026 (early voting 7 July). All 56 seats are contested; 29 needed for a majority. ~2.73 million registered voters, just over half Malay.

**Consensus expected outcome:** BN is the near-unanimous frontrunner to retain Johor, cited by virtually every analyst (Oh Ei Sun, Hutchinson/ISEAS, Azmi Hassan, Syaza Shukri, Mazlan Ali). BN is confident of matching or exceeding its 2022 haul of 40/56 seats.

**"Green wave" question:** Analysts broadly do NOT expect a southern replication of the northern PN/PAS Malay Islamist surge. PN's Malay support appears to have plateaued, and its best hope is confined to rural interior seats around Muhyiddin's Pagoh base.

**Why BN has the edge:**
- Strong incumbency, united machinery, consistent ~600,000-vote turnout across three elections
- Onn Hafiz's popularity + economic momentum (Johor-Singapore SEZ, data centres)
- **PN's implosion:** PAS formally ended cooperation with Bersatu (8 June), directed supporters to back BN in the 23 seats PN isn't contesting, and Bersatu is severely weakened (possibly facing extinction in Johor)

**Key risks/uncertainties:** Onn set a high bar (below 40 seats = failure); a crowded multi-cornered field (BN solo, PH solo, PN 33 seats, new Bersama party) could fragment votes; the PAS-BN linkage risks pushing non-Malay voters to PH. Low turnout favors BN — PH would need >85% turnout to seriously limit BN. The 21 seats that flipped in 2022 are the decisive battlegrounds.

**Data gaps:** No prediction markets exist for this election, and no reliable public horse-race polls with seat projections were found — analysis is expert/qualitative. All data is current as of 1 July 2026, 10 days before polling.

The structured findings have been submitted via the StructuredOutput tool with 16 key facts, 19 data points, and 16 sources.

sources.title: 2026 Johor state election - Wikipedia
sources.snippet: Election expected 11 July 2026; dissolution announced 1 June 2026; all 56 seats contested; determines whether BN under Onn Hafiz retains power.

sources.title: Why BN Believes It Can Go Solo and Still Win Johor
sources.snippet: Dr Oh Ei Sun: Johor BN confident of repeating 40-seat win or exceeding it; no southern green wave foreseen; PN's best hope in rural interior near Muhyiddin's Pagoh base.

sources.title: The 2026 Johor Election: Advantage Barisan Nasional?
sources.snippet: Francis Hutchinson (ISEAS): BN enjoys tailwinds, consistent ~600,000 votes, PN implosion; but Onn set bar high at 40 seats; risks from crowded field.

sources.title: BN Enters Johor Polls With Edge As Parties Eye GE16 Momentum, Experts Say
sources.snippet: Azmi Hassan and Syaza Shukri: BN frontrunner; PN could struggle to retain seats; strong PN showing would signal Malay green wave not yet peaked.

sources.title: PAS call to back Barisan Nasional in Johor state polls could backfire, say analysts
sources.snippet: PN contests only 33 seats; PAS directs supporters to back BN in non-contested seats; risk of galvanising non-Malay vote for PH.

sources.title: Onn Hafiz becomes Johor polls' biggest asset and biggest target
sources.snippet: Election evolving into a contest centred on Onn Hafiz; his visibility an asset but also a target letting opposition frame it as a referendum.

sources.title: PN internal strain could boost BN prospects in Johor state poll, analyst says
sources.snippet: ILHAM Centre's Hisomuddin Bakar: PAS-Bersatu friction erodes PN cohesion, hands BN tactical advantage; 47.6% of voters aged 18-39.

sources.title: BN-PAS quiet backing may signal an emerging Malay vote strategy in Johor polls
sources.snippet: Mazlan Ali: BN clear favourite; PH needs turnout above 85% to limit BN; PAS strategy to avoid splitting Malay vote.

sources.title: PN bets on hung Johor assembly
sources.snippet: PN chairman Sahruddin Jamal predicts hung assembly; Bersatu severely weakened after PAS ended cooperation; defending Bukit Kepong (710-vote margin) and Endau.

sources.title: Bersatu voters could back PH out of spite for PAS, says analyst
sources.snippet: Azmi Hassan: Bersatu voters may back PH out of spite for PAS; Ahmad Fauzi (USM) counters DAP stigma prevents this.

sources.title: These 21 Johor seats decided two elections - Will they decide a third on July 11?
sources.snippet: 21 seats flipped in 2022 are key battlegrounds; turnout collapsed from 84.51% (2018) to 54.92% (2022).

sources.title: Johor State Election Nomination Closes With Heavyweights Set For Contests
sources.snippet: Nomination closed 27 June; 2,727,926 registered voters; BN/PH all 56 seats, PN 33, Bersama 15, MUDA-PSM 5; Onn Hafiz defends Machap.

sources.title: 国阵国盟似联手 希盟柔州选情吃紧 (BN, PN appear to cooperate; PH campaign under pressure)
sources.snippet: 172 candidates (down from 239 in 2022); 14 straight, 27 three-cornered, 12 four-cornered, 3 five-cornered fights; PN may ally with BN in a hung outcome.

sources.title: 弃战23席，国盟策略或让国阵在柔选占优势 (PN abandons 23 seats, strategy may advantage BN)
sources.snippet: PN withdrew from 23 seats (13 BN-held, 9 PH-held, 1 UMNO), suggesting tacit seat allocation with BN.

sources.title: Johor and Negeri Sembilan state elections are double trouble for Anwar
sources.snippet: Johor (11 July) and Negeri Sembilan (1 August) elections could undercut Anwar's PH if it loses; first major test of Madani government.

sources.title: EC issues 24,677 postal ballot papers for 16th Johor Election
sources.snippet: Election Commission issued 24,677 postal ballots across all 56 constituencies.

data_points.metric: BN consistent vote total across last three elections
Turnout PH reportedly needs to limit BN majority
Sahruddin Jamal's 2022 winning margin in Bukit Kepong
